title: sempervivum montanum subsp. montanum
intro: Sempervivum montanum subsp. montanum, commonly known as the Mountain Sempervivum or Mountain Houseleek, is a succulent plant species within the Sempervivum genus. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been used to promote overall health and well-being, with potential benefits including reduced stress and improved immune function. Sempervivum montanum subsp. montanum is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to soothe skin irritations and promote tissue repair.
